description: next/ v4 — PHASE-0 ARCHITECTURE FIX: REAL ERP OperationsResponse (Level-3 IST/actual feedback) via the sim-v5 ERP REST API (http://192.168.178.154:38260/api/confirmations), replacing the postgresql/erp-operations-response.json direct-erpdb poller (erpdb.llm_test_v3.order_confirmations — the forbidden direct-DB bypass). Same sourceId (edge SOURCE_IDS unchanged), same profile contract (SMProfile-OperationsResponse). production_order_no = join key (cross-rule join + kgIdProperty; RESPONDS_TO ProductionOrder on the same key). confirmed_qty -> quantity_produced drives the qty_shortfall cross-rule against ProductionOrder.qty_planned. cycle_time_actual -> cycle_time_actual_sec (the real actual cycle time). ts_end -> actual_end_time + completed_at (the REST confirmation's end timestamp is the actual completion time; the old postgres source used confirmation_time for both). recorded_at -> updated_at. /api/confirmations has NO status column, but every confirmation IS a completion -> status bound to the constant 'fertig' ({ const: 'fertig', smAttribute: 'status' }) so the OperationsResponse contract is complete. NOTE 2026-07-12: the three cross-rules that gated on this attribute (qty_shortfall, material_unavailable, late_delivery) are RETIRED — a when-guard over a source-bound constant can never discriminate (see their tombstones and ci/lint-cross-constraints.mjs, which fails the build on any ACTIVE rule with such a guard). The constant stays for contract completeness; it MUST NOT be used as a when-guard operand. full_refresh change-detection (the REST API does not honour ?since — verified) — it-edge dedups via the per-id HWM on production_order_no. filter=null (full confirmation set per the cutover decision). machine_no/machine_id WERE null on the confirmation feed (BDE did not stamp the machine). 2026-07-22: the sim-v5 BDE fix now stamps the ACTUAL machine (machine_no -> machine_ref, recent coverage ~100% measured on .120+.154) and emits cycle_time_planned + good_qty + scrap_qty + planned_machine_no, all now mapped (cycle_time_planned -> cycle_time_planned_sec, good_qty, scrap_qty, planned_machine_no -> planned_machine_ref). This lets per-machine OEE (Performance = cycle_time_planned/cycle_time_actual capped 1.0, Quality = good/(good+scrap)) and the planned!=actual dispatch deviation resolve straight from the confirmation. Schema-Change = Full-Rebuild to reach the running edge/ingest.
